vocabulary.name: Bandsintown API Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Key terms and concepts used in the Bandsintown Public API

terms.definition: A musical performer or band whose tour dates, events, and metadata can be retrieved via the Bandsintown API.
The URL-encoded name of an artist used as a path parameter in API requests. Special characters must be percent-encoded (/ → %252F, ? → %253F, * → %252A, " → %27C).
A required query parameter representing the application ID assigned to a developer or partner by Bandsintown, used for authentication and usage tracking.
A scheduled live music concert or performance associated with one or more artists, including date, venue, ticket links, and lineup information.
The physical location where a concert event takes place, including name, city, region, country, and geographic coordinates.
A ticket purchasing opportunity associated with an event, including the offer type, purchase URL, and availability status.
The list of artists performing at a given event.
The number of Bandsintown fans who have chosen to track a particular artist for event notifications.
The total number of confirmed upcoming events for a given artist.
The MusicBrainz Identifier, a unique string that identifies an artist in the MusicBrainz open music encyclopedia.
A query parameter for filtering events. Accepted values are 'upcoming', 'past', 'all', or a date range in the format 'YYYY-MM-DD,YYYY-MM-DD'.
The ISO 8601 date-time when tickets for an event become available for purchase.
The ISO 8601 date-time of the scheduled event start.
